description_long: This is a realistic model of a crab, featuring a robust, oval-shaped carapace with a hard, mottled brown and gray exoskeleton. The crab has two prominent, asymmetrical claws (chelae), with one claw significantly larger than the other, both displaying a smooth, slightly glossy surface. Its body is supported by eight segmented walking legs, each jointed and tapering to a point, with a slightly rougher texture than the claws. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the carapace, providing a wide field of vision. Subtle color gradations from brown to gray and hints of blue are visible, especially on the claws and legs. The overall appearance suggests a species adapted to both aquatic and terrestrial environments, commonly found in coastal or urban park settings.
description: A lifelike crab with a hard, mottled brown-gray shell, two large claws (one much bigger), and eight jointed legs. Its eyes are on short stalks, and the surface is slightly glossy with subtle color variations.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the crab's broad carapace, both claws raised, and the full spread of its legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pronounced size difference between the two claws and the curvature of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of the legs and the back of the carapace.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view emphasizing the crab's body segmentation and the articulation of the legs.
